subject: Sociology
title: Control Theory
description: A short lesson to help you apply Control Theory.

hook.text: Most theories ask why people commit crime. Travis Hirschi asked the opposite: why don't more people?

buildup.text: Control theory argues that social bonds prevent crime. When bonds weaken, deviance becomes more likely.

discovery.text: Four bonds matter: attachment to others, commitment to goals, involvement in activities, and belief in rules.

twist.text: This explains why employed, married people with community ties commit less crime — they have more to lose.

climax.text: Crime isn't caused by special motives. It happens when the normal restraints that hold people back break down.

punchline.text: We stay good because bonds hold us in.

quiz.question: What does control theory focus on?
options: Why most people don't commit crime despite opportunity
